text:
There once was a young girl named Lily who loved to collect feathers. Every bird she saw stopped to pluck a feather. One morning, her favorite bird, a lovely blue eagle, had flown far away. The next day Lily noticed an unusual feather on her bed—the eagle's distinctive plumage.

With sadness, Lily went outside to see the eagle resting on a nearby branch. She didn't move. Lily felt so lonely she started knitting. Each feather lace edged her garment brought her another dear companion bird.

When she finished, she embroidered the eagle's feather into its hat. The eagle came closer, staring.

That night, the eagle flew into Lily's window. She scooped it up, careful not to lose its feather.

In gratitude, Lily started giving the feather to friends. They loved it.

Years went by. Lily finally saved enough to fly the eagle into her hometown. She brought it to the local museum.
